seo.title: Tennessee CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Tennessee - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer CNA license to tennessee, tennessee CNA endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Dept of Health
board.mailing_address: Tennessee Department of Health, Health Care Facilities, 665 Mainstream Drive, Nashville, TN 37243

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Tennessee.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Tennessee-approved Nurse Aide Training Program.

steps.title: Register for the Competency Exam
steps.description: Register for and pass the Tennessee Nurse Aide Competency Examination, administered by Prometric.

steps.title: Access the CNA365 Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the official CNA365 system at cnaregistry.prometric.com to begin your application process.

steps.title: Create or Log In to Your Account
steps.description: Create a new user account if you are a first-time applicant or log in with your existing credentials on CNA365.

steps.title: Submit Your Application
steps.description: Complete and submit the initial enrollment application via CNA365, providing all required personal information, training details, and exam results.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Track the status of your application through the CNA365 portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Tennessee Department of Health.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Tennessee CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: No, fingerprints are generally not required for CNA endorsement in Tennessee.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in TN.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Tennessee.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for Certified Nursing Assistant licensing in Tennessee, navigate to the CNA365 system, which is managed by Prometric on behalf of the Tennessee Department of Health.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Tennessee

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the CNA365 portal, click 'New User' to create an account, or 'Login' if you already have one, then proceed to select the appropriate application type (e.g., initial enrollment, reciprocity).
